1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b.product_description: Use of AI tools in business processes and delivery

prediction.review_text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI, it's essential to focus on the challenges and practicalities of effectively using AI tools that can truly transform our businesses. At CodingRippler, we've seen firsthand how integrating AI into our business processes can streamline operations and enhance delivery consistency. A crucial part of our strategy involves leveraging automation platforms like n8n and Zapier to connect various workflows. For instance, automating the process from Gmail to Google Sheets using AI extraction not only saves valuable time but also minimizes errors, allowing teams to concentrate on higher-value tasks. Our commitment lies in creating reliable and repeatable solutions. We provide scenario-based instructions to ensure our clients are well-prepared for post-launch changes. It's not merely about adopting the latest technology; it's about establishing a sustainable framework that supports growth and adaptability. I encourage fellow founders and tech professionals to explore how AI can be effectively utilized in your operations. Let's share our experiences and insights to foster a community of innovation and support. Together, we can harness the power of AI to drive meaningful change in our businesses. #AI #Automation #EffectiveUse #CommunitySupport

actual.review_text:
AI tools aren't the edge.

The instructions are.

Everyone has access to the same AI tools now.

What makes the difference is how you instruct them.

At CodingRippler, we're heavily investing in building scenario-based instructions for our engineering team.

→ Check this before delivery
→ Flag this if it breaks
→ Don't miss this

AI runs through them. Every time.

The tool didn't change. The way we use it did.

Faster delivery. Consistent quality.

If you've been waiting to build because "agencies cost too much" that's changed.

 

#AI #Startup #Founder #Entrepreneur #SmallBusiness #Agency

1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b.product_description: Collaboration with Indian technical talent for project success

prediction.review_text: In today's technology landscape, the collaboration with Indian technical talent stands out as a powerful driver for project success and innovation. At CodingRippler, we have experienced the transformative impact of leveraging local expertise, which not only enhances our project outcomes but also enriches the diversity of thought within our teams. Our skilled professionals bring a unique blend of technical depth and cultural insights, allowing us to create customized solutions that resonate across various sectors. By utilizing no-code and low-code orchestration tools like n8n, Make, and Zapier, we streamline operations, enabling businesses to adapt quickly to evolving demands. This partnership fosters a culture of problem-solving and adaptability, bringing diverse perspectives to the forefront. Together, we can tackle complex challenges and contribute to a more productive tech ecosystem that benefits all stakeholders. Let's celebrate the strengths of our teams and harness the power of collaboration to drive meaningful change in our industry. Together, we can build solutions that not only address today's challenges but also pave the way for a more inclusive and innovative future. #DiversityInTech #IndianTalent #Collaboration #Automation #NoCode #TechForGood

As we navigate the ever-evolving landscape of technology, I want to take a moment to highlight the incredible value of collaborating with Indian technical talent. At CodingRippler, we've seen firsthand how leveraging local expertise can drive project success and innovation. 

Our team embodies a unique blend of technical depth and cultural understanding, which allows us to create tailored solutions that resonate with diverse markets. By integrating no-code and low-code orchestration tools like n8n, Make, and Zapier, we not only streamline operations but also empower businesses to adapt quickly to changing demands. 

Moreover, this collaboration fosters inclusivity and diversity in tech, bringing fresh perspectives and ideas to the table. As we work together to tackle complex challenges, we create a more equitable tech ecosystem that benefits everyone. 

Let’s continue to champion the strengths of our diverse teams and harness the power of collaboration to drive meaningful change in our industry. Together, we can build solutions that not only meet the needs of today but also pave the way for a brighter, more inclusive future. 

#DiversityInTech #Collaboration #IndianTalent #Automation #NoCode #TechForGood

actual.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

351405ab-deb1-45b5-810c-d32798c95f40.product_description: Job market communication for software engineers and product managers

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can feel overwhelming, especially in today’s fast-paced tech landscape. I want to share a few insights that have helped me and many of my peers find success in our careers. 

1. **Tailor Your Communication**: When applying for roles, ensure your resume and cover letter speak directly to the job description. Highlight relevant experiences and skills that align with the company's needs. 

2. **Network Authentically**: Building genuine connections can open doors. Attend industry events, engage in online communities, and don’t hesitate to reach out to professionals in your field. You never know who might be willing to help you land your next opportunity. 

3. **Showcase Your Work**: Create a portfolio that demonstrates your skills. Whether it’s through GitHub repositories, personal projects, or contributions to open-source, having tangible examples of your work can set you apart from other candidates. 

4. **Stay Updated**: The tech industry evolves rapidly. Make it a habit to learn about new tools, frameworks, and best practices. This not only enhances your skill set but also shows potential employers your commitment to growth. 

5. **Seek Feedback**: Don’t shy away from asking for constructive criticism on your applications or interviews. It’s an invaluable way to improve and refine your approach. 

Remember, the journey may have its ups and downs, but resilience and adaptability are key. Let’s support each other in this community as we navigate our career paths together. Feel free to share your own tips or experiences in the comments below! #CareerDevelopment #JobMarket #SoftwareEngineering #ProductManagement #CommunitySupport

actual.review_text:
Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

🚀 As we navigate the challenging landscape of career transitions in the tech industry, I want to shine a light on the incredible talent within our community, especially those who may be facing layoffs. If you or someone you know is a skilled engineer seeking new opportunities, please reach out! 🤝

At Microsoft, we are actively looking for passionate individuals who can contribute to our innovative projects. Whether your expertise lies in backend development, AI, or robotics, there’s a place for you here. Let’s connect and support each other during this critical time. Remember, every referral counts and can significantly impact someone's career journey.

I also want to emphasize the importance of mentorship and community support during these transitions. Sharing experiences and guidance can make a world of difference. Feel free to drop a comment or DM me if you need assistance or know someone who does. Together, we can empower our community and help talented engineers find their next big opportunity! 💪 #CareerDevelopment #Referrals #Mentorship #TechCommunity #Microsoft #EdTech

actual.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

prediction.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey in the tech industry, I’m continually reminded of the invaluable lessons learned through mentorship and shared experiences. Each interaction, whether with a seasoned engineer or a fellow startup founder, has shaped my approach to problem-solving and innovation. 

At Microsoft, I’ve had the privilege of collaborating with some of the brightest minds in cloud infrastructure and AI, and these experiences have reinforced the importance of community in our field. It’s not just about individual achievements; it’s about lifting each other up and sharing knowledge to foster collective growth. 

I encourage everyone in our network to seek out mentorship opportunities and to be open to learning from those around you. Let’s continue to support one another, celebrate our wins, and create an environment where everyone feels empowered to thrive. 

If you’re looking for guidance or want to share your insights, feel free to reach out. Together, we can navigate the complexities of our careers and drive meaningful change in the tech landscape!

actual.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow

Reflecting on my journey in the video game industry, I am deeply grateful for the experiences that have shaped my career. My time at Ubisoft not only taught me the intricacies of game development but also instilled the importance of collaboration and innovation in a fast-paced environment.

One of the most valuable lessons I learned was the significance of resilience. Game development can be unpredictable, with tight deadlines and evolving project scopes. Embracing challenges and adapting to change became essential skills that I carry with me to my current role at Microsoft.

Mentorship has been a cornerstone of my growth. I was fortunate to work alongside talented individuals who guided me through complex projects and encouraged my professional development. This experience has fueled my passion for giving back to the community through mentorship initiatives, where I strive to support others in their career journeys.

As I continue to navigate the tech landscape, I remain committed to fostering a culture of learning and collaboration. Whether through sharing insights on developer tools or engaging in discussions about the future of technology, I believe in the power of community support.

actual.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar

I've successfully completed the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" from LinkedIn Learning, a learning path which consists of over 17 hours of C++ theory and in-depth exercises!

My favorite module of the learning path was and still is "Web Servers and APIs Using C++", by Troy Miles.

The module was outstanding, I've learnt how to create and automate Web Servers with C++, deploy resources using Docker, include HTML5, CSS3 and PHP elements and, last but not least, integrate external resources such as MongoDB to the Web Servers.

I'm very grateful for everything that I've learnt and this is just the start!

I recommend the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" for every C++ passionate programmer and for everyone else who would like to enrichen their C++ knowledge.

Link to the LinkedIn Learning Path: 

Thank you, everyone!

#developer #docker #programming #cplusplus #objectorientedprogramming #linkedinlearning

As we navigate the ever-evolving landscape of technology, it's essential to prioritize our professional development and learning strategies. Whether you're just starting your career or looking to make a significant transition, embracing a growth mindset can be your greatest asset. 

Here are a few strategies that have helped me and many others in our journeys:

1. **Continuous Learning**: In our field, staying updated with the latest tools and technologies is crucial. Platforms like Microsoft Learn and Coursera offer a wealth of resources that can help you expand your skill set at your own pace.

2. **Mentorship**: Seek out mentors who can provide guidance and insights based on their experiences. I’ve found that having someone to discuss challenges with can provide clarity and motivation.

3. **Networking**: Engage with your peers and industry leaders. Join communities, attend meetups, or participate in online forums. Sharing experiences and knowledge can open doors to new opportunities.

4. **Practical Application**: Don’t just learn—apply what you learn in real-world scenarios. Contributing to open-source projects or taking on side projects can enhance your understanding and showcase your skills.

5. **Emotional Resilience**: Remember that setbacks are part of the journey. Building emotional resilience will help you navigate challenges and maintain your motivation.

Let’s continue to support one another in our professional journeys. What strategies have worked for you? Share your thoughts below! #CareerDevelopment #ContinuousLearning #Mentorship #Networking #Resilience

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

prediction.review_text:
Reflecting on my career journey, I recognize how pivotal transitions have shaped my path. From my early days at Adobe to my current role at Microsoft, each step has been a learning experience filled with challenges and triumphs. Navigating the tech landscape is no easy feat, especially as AI and automation rapidly transform industries.

A significant lesson I've learned is the importance of resilience and overcoming impostor syndrome. There were times I faced setbacks—whether troubleshooting complex systems or adapting to new technologies. Yet, with each challenge, I discovered opportunities for growth and innovation, which helped build my confidence.

Mentorship has been crucial in my journey. I’ve been fortunate to have mentors guiding me through tough times, and now I strive to pay it forward by supporting the next generation of tech talent. I encourage everyone to seek out mentors and also to be mentors themselves. Sharing experiences can profoundly impact someone's career.

As we embrace the future of work, let’s prioritize mental health and community support. We are all in this together, and fostering a culture of inclusivity and collaboration will drive our industry forward. Here’s to embracing change, overcoming challenges, and supporting each other through our career transitions! #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship #Resilience #ImpostorSyndrome #CommunitySupport

actual.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe

I will also complain to banking ombudsman and to Dept of Telecom (DOT) for misusing our contact number to spam us with marketing calls. Shameful

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the tech job market can feel overwhelming, especially with the rapid pace of change in our industry. As someone who has been on both sides of the hiring process, I want to share some insights that might help you in your job search. 

First, leverage your network. Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and even connections you’ve made at events like Microsoft Ignite. Personal referrals can significantly boost your chances of landing interviews. 

Second, tailor your resume and cover letter for each application. Highlight specific skills and experiences that align with the job description. Remember, it’s not just about listing your technical skills; it’s about demonstrating how you can solve problems for the company. 

Additionally, don’t shy away from showcasing your soft skills. Resilience, adaptability, and teamwork are just as crucial in tech roles as technical expertise. Employers are looking for candidates who can thrive in dynamic environments and contribute to a positive team culture. 

Lastly, take care of your mental health during this process. Job searching can be stressful, so make sure to set aside time for self-care and reach out for support when needed. Remember, you’re not alone in this journey. Let’s continue to support each other in our career transitions. 

If you have any tips or experiences to share, I’d love to hear them in the comments below! Together, we can navigate this landscape and find opportunities that align with our passions and skills.

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

prediction.review_text:
Navigating higher education in the U.S. can be both a transformative and challenging journey for international students. Here are some insights based on my experiences:

1. **Research Thoroughly**: Familiarize yourself with the educational landscape, including differences in academic systems and cultural nuances. Identify programs that align with your career aspirations and offer strong support for international students.

2. **Network Effectively**: Utilize platforms like LinkedIn to connect with alumni and current students. Their insights can guide you through your journey. Don’t hesitate to seek mentorship; many professionals are eager to share their experiences.

3. **Acknowledge Emotional Challenges**: It’s normal to feel overwhelmed or experience impostor syndrome during this transition. Engage with communities that support international students and seek help when needed. Remember, you’re not alone.

4. **Embrace Diversity**: Keep an open mind and appreciate the learning opportunities that come with studying in a new country. Each experience, whether positive or challenging, contributes to your personal and professional growth.

5. **Consider Financial Implications**: Be aware of the financial burdens that higher education can impose. Look for scholarships, assistantships, and financial aid options that can ease the burden.

Let’s support one another on this journey. If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Together, we can navigate the path to success in higher education and beyond! This post emphasizes resilience and the importance of community support, aligning with the challenges faced by international students in their educational pursuits.

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

actual.review_text:
Estatisticas mundias estimam que "aprox. 70% das pessoas irão vivenciar sindrome de impostor pelo menos uma vez na vida" ( Essa experiência afeta principalmente as mulheres e, caso não identificada e persistente, pode ter impacto considerável em nossa carreira. 
A comunidade Brasil do The WIT Network faz um convite a você para participar de um bate papo online sobre o tema. Venha conhecer mais sobre a Sindrome de Impostor, bem como ouvir as estorias e dicas de profissionais talentosos que vivenciaram e superaram essa experiencia. 
Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women. 
Non identified cases or persistently experiencing Imposter Syndrome may affect your career progression. With this in mind, the Brazilian Community of The WIT Network invites you to join an online roundtable about the subject. Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

As a Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft, I'm continually inspired by the transformative potential of AI in education and skills development. It's remarkable to see how AI applications are enhancing learning experiences and making them more accessible to diverse communities. 

In our fast-paced tech landscape, leveraging AI can effectively bridge gaps in education by providing personalized learning paths and real-time feedback that empower both students and educators. However, we must also recognize the resilience and adaptability required from both educators and learners to successfully integrate AI into educational frameworks. 

Fostering a supportive community around these challenges is essential. Together, we can cultivate an environment where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. This journey is not solely about technology; it’s about ensuring equitable access to knowledge and skills that can transform lives. 

Let’s continue to share insights, resources, and experiences that emphasize the practical applications of AI in education while also addressing the emotional aspects of professional growth and the uncertainties we face. By promoting mentorship and community support, we can drive innovation and ensure that technology serves as a tool for empowerment and growth. 

What are your thoughts on the role of AI in reshaping education and how we can collectively navigate the uncertainties it brings? I’d love to hear your experiences and ideas!

In the last week I decided to close a cycle in my life. I finished a fantastic journey of 15 years working on research and development at Eldorado.

Thank you also to all partners and customers that gave me the chance to learn with you.
Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

actual.review_text:
If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Last year I embarked on a mission to help people understand more about Data Engineering. I had the opportunity to talk to almost 30 people about data engineering and share some insights about the profession with them.

I've decided again to open some slots on my schedule this year! :)
So, if you are into Data Engineering and you are interested to join Factored or you want to get some guidance on Data Engineering, please reach out to me. Also, if you know anyone interested in this topic, please tag them so we can get in touch! :)

You can find the link to grab a slot on my calendar in the comments :)
#factored #dataengineering #data #latinamerica

prediction.review_text:
As we navigate the integration of AI into our workflows, it's essential to discuss how technologies like SAP S/4HANA can transform financial processes through AI capabilities. While specific functionalities like COGS splits and Account Based COPA reporting are vital, we should also consider the broader implications of AI in enhancing accessibility and efficiency across various sectors. AI can help automate routine tasks, reduce errors, and provide deeper insights into financial data, ultimately benefiting all users. 

For those interested in exploring these capabilities, I recommend activating free test systems via the SAP CAL (Cloud Appliance Library). This hands-on experience allows you to experiment with AI functionalities in a safe setting. Additionally, I encourage you to engage with community resources, such as SAP notes and blogs, that offer step-by-step guidance on implementing these features. Together, we can make these powerful tools accessible and actionable for everyone. Let's share our experiences and learn from one another as we embrace the exciting potential of AI in finance and beyond! #SAP #S4HANA #AI #Finance #CommunityLearning

actual.review_text:
Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

actual.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

actual.review_text:
I rarely reshare content, but this one deeply resonated.

Simon Sinek nails what it means to truly lead. He also nails what it means to truly love.

It's a rare thing that someone can point out something so obviously true but yet so fleeting that you immediately wanna say “This makes so much sense how did I not see this”.

This video also made me very aware of all the little things I am doing, I am not doing and I should be doing. Especially concerning showing appreciation for certain people. 

This video made me reach out to a person from a very vulnerable place and it rekindled a deep connection.

What are some small ways in which you can better show your love / leadership (pick one)?

actual.review_text:
Just finished the course “Learning React.js”! #reactjs
Made my first web app UI with React.

Look out for these key concepts:
Creating and rendering React elements,
Representing UI elements with React components,
adding props to display dynamic data,
Building components with JS class,
Using React states to re-render and change state of components,
bind event functions to UI elements with state variables,
and Conditional rendering of components.

JSX is a boon! Makes writing your own components much easier and makes my source much cleaner.

Eve Porcello does a great job of breaking down React concepts, and a hands-on example to help you build on your own.

3. **Explore AI Integrations**: Consider incorporating AI features! Tools like TensorFlow can add intelligent functionalities to your web applications, making them more interactive and user-friendly. This integration not only enhances user experience but also addresses real-world challenges, showcasing the transformative potential of technology in education and beyond.

4. **Prioritize Security**: Always keep security at the forefront, especially when dealing with user data. Implement best practices such as input validation and secure authentication to protect your applications and build trust within your user community.

5. **Engage with the Community**: Share your experiences and learn from others! Platforms like GitHub and Stack Overflow are invaluable for finding solutions and connecting with fellow developers. Remember, mentorship and community support are key to personal and professional growth, especially in navigating the emotional aspects of career transitions.

NIKE SALUTES CHANDRAYAN

#Nike continues to be a #brand thats always ahead of the curve in terms of its #marketing #advertising #content and even embracing #technology for connecting with its consumers 
It’s about having a vigilant #digitalmarketing team who’s continually looking to engage with audiences across the world.

The latest one being a brilliant piece in #momentmarketing that salutes the “CHANDRAYAN”

More often than not it’s just brilliant visuals that this #brand uses with its iconic swoosh that speaks for itself.

Saluting the spirit of #India and sports, Nike sure gets full marks for this one yet again!

Do you agree?

actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

actual_review_data.review_text:
AI tools aren't the edge.

The instructions are.

Everyone has access to the same AI tools now.

What makes the difference is how you instruct them.

At CodingRippler, we're heavily investing in building scenario-based instructions for our engineering team.

→ Check this before delivery
→ Flag this if it breaks
→ Don't miss this

AI runs through them. Every time.

The tool didn't change. The way we use it did.

Faster delivery. Consistent quality.

If you've been waiting to build because "agencies cost too much" that's changed.

 

#AI #Startup #Founder #Entrepreneur #SmallBusiness #Agency

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to navigate the rapidly evolving landscape of AI, it's crucial to focus on practical applications that can drive real impact in our businesses. At CodingRippler, we've seen firsthand how integrating AI tools into business processes can streamline operations and enhance delivery. 

One of the most effective strategies we've implemented is using automation platforms like n8n and Zapier to connect various tools and workflows. For instance, automating the flow from Gmail to Google Sheets using AI extraction not only saves time but also reduces errors, allowing teams to focus on higher-value tasks. 

The key is to ensure that these solutions are reliable and repeatable. We emphasize scenario-based instructions to help our clients plan for post-launch changes effectively. It's not just about deploying the latest technology; it's about creating a sustainable framework that supports growth and adaptability. 

I encourage fellow founders and tech professionals to explore how AI can be leveraged in your operations. Let's share our experiences and insights to foster a community of innovation and support. actual_review_data.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

313325f0-0334-45f7-93b3-e65adcc597e9_rev.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

actual_review_data.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

actual_review_data.review_text: Anyone looking for a sr./principal level software engineer, sr. level product/program manager (either greater Seattle area or remote)? With the market still being crazy-competitive, some of my friends are still looking for a job after the big lay-off wave. Please ping me and I'll share their resume/profile.
actual_review_data.product_description: Job market communication for software engineers and product managers

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the job market as a software engineer or product manager can feel overwhelming, especially in today’s fast-paced tech landscape. I want to share a few insights that have helped me and many of my peers find success in our careers. 

1. **Tailor Your Communication**: When applying for roles, ensure your resume and cover letter speak directly to the job description. Highlight relevant experiences and skills that align with the company's needs. 

2. **Network Authentically**: Building genuine connections can open doors. Attend industry events, engage in online communities, and don’t hesitate to reach out to professionals in your field. You never know who might be willing to help you land your next opportunity. 

3. **Showcase Your Work**: Create a portfolio that demonstrates your skills. Whether it’s through GitHub repositories, personal projects, or contributions to open-source, having tangible examples of your work can set you apart from other candidates. 

4. **Stay Updated**: The tech industry evolves rapidly. Make it a habit to learn about new tools, frameworks, and best practices. This not only enhances your skill set but also shows potential employers your commitment to growth. 

5. **Seek Feedback**: Don’t shy away from asking for constructive criticism on your applications or interviews. It’s an invaluable way to improve and refine your approach. 

Remember, the journey may have its ups and downs, but resilience and adaptability are key. Let’s support each other in this community as we navigate our career paths together. Feel free to share your own tips or experiences in the comments below! #CareerDevelopment #JobMarket #SoftwareEngineering #ProductManagement #CommunitySupport

Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

I encourage everyone to explore these offerings, especially if you're considering a career transition or simply want to enhance your skills. If you know of any great resources or courses, feel free to share in the comments! #ProgrammingEducation #TechForAll #CommunitySupport

actual_review_data.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

54c19b05-7985-47be-b2f2-6a2f8360cf89_rev.product_description: Referrals and support for talented engineers seeking new opportunities

actual_review_data.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

Reflecting on my journey in the tech industry, I’m continually reminded of the invaluable lessons learned through mentorship and shared experiences. Each interaction, whether with a seasoned engineer or a fellow startup founder, has shaped my approach to problem-solving and innovation. 

At Microsoft, I’ve had the privilege of collaborating with some of the brightest minds in cloud infrastructure and AI, and these experiences have reinforced the importance of community in our field. It’s not just about individual achievements; it’s about lifting each other up and sharing knowledge to foster collective growth. 

I encourage everyone in our network to seek out mentorship opportunities and to be open to learning from those around you. Let’s continue to support one another, celebrate our wins, and create an environment where everyone feels empowered to thrive. 

If you’re looking for guidance or want to share your insights, feel free to reach out. Together, we can navigate the complexities of our careers and drive meaningful change in the tech landscape!

actual_review_data.product_description: Challenges in enterprise AI implementations

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to integrate AI into enterprise environments, it's crucial to address the challenges that come with these implementations. One of the most significant hurdles is ensuring that AI systems are not only effective but also align with the existing infrastructure and workflows. This requires a deep understanding of both the technology and the business processes at play. 

From my experience, one of the key strategies to overcome these challenges is to adopt a collaborative approach. Engaging cross-functional teams early in the process can help identify potential roadblocks and ensure that the AI solutions we develop are tailored to meet real-world needs. 

Moreover, we must prioritize transparency and ethical considerations in our AI models. This means being mindful of data privacy, bias, and the overall impact on users. By fostering an environment of open dialogue and continuous learning, we can navigate these complexities more effectively. 

I encourage everyone in our community to share their experiences and insights on this topic. What challenges have you faced in AI implementations, and how have you addressed them? Let's learn from each other and drive innovation together!

3a0a7529-16a1-48d1-b987-c60e4260ad46_rev.product_description: Challenges and discussions in AI and platform engineering

actual_review_data.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow
actual_review_data.product_description: Challenges and discussions in AI and platform engineering

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we continue to navigate the complexities of AI and platform engineering, it's crucial to address the challenges that come with integrating these technologies into our existing systems. One of the key hurdles we face is ensuring that AI solutions are not only effective but also accessible and responsible. 

In my experience working with Azure and Kubernetes, I've seen firsthand how important it is to adopt a scenario-based approach when implementing AI. This means considering the real-world implications of our decisions and how they affect not just our infrastructure, but also our users and the broader community. 

I encourage everyone in our network to share their experiences and insights on this topic. What challenges have you encountered in your AI integration efforts? How have you addressed issues of accessibility and ethical considerations? Let's foster a discussion that not only highlights the technical aspects but also emphasizes the societal impacts of our work. Together, we can drive innovation while ensuring that we are building a future that is inclusive and beneficial for all.

actual_review_data.review_text:
I'm happy to present to open-source enthusiasts and computer science students about the intricacies of OpenTelemetry and how it can be leveraged to build efficient and scalable systems.

I'll also showcase how telemetry data can be fed into a data visualization tool like Grafana Labs' Grafana with the help of the `grafana-lgtm` docker container stack.

Thank you for this opportunity, Răzvan Deaconescu and ROSEdu.
Thank you to everyone who contributes to the open-source space.

7333df73-63cf-4f92-b724-caefc9171b63_rev.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

actual_review_data.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar
actual_review_data.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey in the video game industry, I often find myself grateful for the experiences that have shaped my career. Working at Ubisoft taught me not only the intricacies of game development but also the importance of collaboration and innovation in a fast-paced environment. 

One of the most valuable lessons I learned was the significance of resilience. Game development can be unpredictable, with tight deadlines and evolving project scopes. Embracing challenges and adapting to change became essential skills that I carry with me to my current role at Microsoft. 

Mentorship played a crucial role in my growth. I was fortunate to work alongside talented individuals who guided me through complex projects and encouraged my professional development. This experience has fueled my passion for giving back to the community through mentorship initiatives, where I strive to support others in their career journeys. 

As I continue to navigate the tech landscape, I remain committed to fostering a culture of learning and collaboration, whether through sharing insights on developer tools or engaging in discussions about the future of technology. 

To anyone in the gaming industry or considering a transition into tech, remember that every challenge is an opportunity for growth. Let's connect and support each other in our journeys! #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship #GameDevelopment #TechCommunity

7333df73-63cf-4f92-b724-caefc9171b63_rev.product_description: Learning paths in programming and software development

actual_review_data.review_text:
I've successfully completed the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" from LinkedIn Learning, a learning path which consists of over 17 hours of C++ theory and in-depth exercises!

My favorite module of the learning path was and still is "Web Servers and APIs Using C++", by Troy Miles.

The module was outstanding, I've learnt how to create and automate Web Servers with C++, deploy resources using Docker, include HTML5, CSS3 and PHP elements and, last but not least, integrate external resources such as MongoDB to the Web Servers.

I'm very grateful for everything that I've learnt and this is just the start!

I recommend the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" for every C++ passionate programmer and for everyone else who would like to enrichen their C++ knowledge.

Link to the LinkedIn Learning Path: 

Thank you, everyone!

#developer #docker #programming #cplusplus #objectorientedprogramming #linkedinlearning

actual_review_data.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.
actual_review_data.product_description: Professional development and learning strategies

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As we navigate the ever-evolving landscape of technology, it's essential to prioritize our professional development and learning strategies. Whether you're just starting your career or looking to make a significant transition, embracing a growth mindset can be your greatest asset. 

Here are a few strategies that have helped me and many others in our journeys:

1. **Continuous Learning**: In our field, staying updated with the latest tools and technologies is crucial. Platforms like Microsoft Learn and Coursera offer a wealth of resources that can help you expand your skill set at your own pace.

2. **Mentorship**: Seek out mentors who can provide guidance and insights based on their experiences. I’ve found that having someone to discuss challenges with can provide clarity and motivation.

3. **Networking**: Engage with your peers and industry leaders. Join communities, attend meetups, or participate in online forums. Sharing experiences and knowledge can open doors to new opportunities.

4. **Practical Application**: Don’t just learn—apply what you learn in real-world scenarios. Contributing to open-source projects or taking on side projects can enhance your understanding and showcase your skills.

5. **Emotional Resilience**: Remember that setbacks are part of the journey. Building emotional resilience will help you navigate challenges and maintain your motivation.

Let’s continue to support one another in our professional journeys. What strategies have worked for you? Share your thoughts below! #CareerDevelopment #ContinuousLearning #Mentorship #Networking #Resilience

actual_review_data.review_text: Cannot agree more, the last two weeks of caregiver leave I received was super helpful for me to take care of my father. Over and above the immense support from my manager & LT during this time ensured that despite this being unplanned I did not receive a single call/ping from business & my team! I was able to focus on my father's health only. #microsoftlife #microsoftculture
actual_review_data.product_description: Support and effectiveness of caregiver leave in a workplace

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As a software engineer at Microsoft, I've had the privilege of experiencing firsthand the impact of caregiver leave on both personal and professional levels. When I took my caregiver leave recently, it was a time filled with challenges but also profound learning. It reinforced my belief that a supportive workplace culture is essential for fostering resilience and adaptability among employees. 

Caregiver leave isn't just a policy; it's a commitment to inclusivity and understanding. It allows us to prioritize our loved ones while ensuring that we can return to our roles ready to contribute effectively. I’ve seen how this support can lead to increased morale and productivity, as employees feel valued and understood. 

I encourage all organizations to reflect on their caregiver leave policies and consider how they can enhance support for their teams. Let's continue to advocate for a workplace that not only recognizes the importance of work-life balance but actively promotes it. Together, we can create environments where everyone feels empowered to thrive, both at work and at home. 

#DiversityAndInclusion #CaregiverLeave #WorkplaceCulture #MentalHealth #Support #Microsoft

5b792818-ac34-4256-97f8-cdce43bde7f0_rev.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

actual_review_data.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe
actual_review_data.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As I reflect on my career journey, I’m reminded of the pivotal transitions that have shaped my path. From my early days at Adobe to my current role at Microsoft, each step has been a learning experience filled with challenges and triumphs. Navigating the tech landscape is no easy feat, especially in an era where AI and automation are rapidly transforming industries. 

One of the most significant lessons I've learned is the importance of resilience. There were times when I faced setbacks, whether it was troubleshooting complex systems or adapting to new technologies. But with each challenge, I found opportunities for growth and innovation. 

Mentorship played a crucial role in my journey. I’ve been fortunate to have mentors who guided me, and now I strive to pay it forward by supporting the next generation of tech talent. I encourage everyone to seek out mentors and also to be a mentor yourself. Sharing experiences and insights can make a world of difference in someone’s career. 

As we continue to embrace the future of work, let’s remember to prioritize mental health and community support. We are all in this together, and fostering a culture of inclusivity and collaboration will drive our industry forward. Here’s to embracing change and supporting each other through our career transitions! #CareerDevelopment #Mentorship #Resilience #AI #CommunitySupport

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8_rev.product_description: Customer experience with financial services and marketing practices

actual_review_data.review_text: HDFC Bank Your representatives call multiple times a week asking me if I want to increase my credit limit . If I tell them to not call me again about it , they just ignore it. Called me twice today about it. Why are you pestering people about it? Might as well get rid of HDFC credit cards & account due to the insane amount of marketing calls. None of them seem to be able to mark a customer as "not interested" and not call again. Really disappointed with you. I will also complain to banking ombudsman and to Dept of Telecom (DOT) for misusing our contact number to spam us with marketing calls. Shameful

At Microsoft, we’re leveraging tools like SharePoint Syntex to automate content classification and extraction, which not only saves time but also improves accuracy in handling vast amounts of data. 

The integration of AI into our processes is not just about efficiency; it’s about democratizing access to information and empowering teams to focus on strategic initiatives rather than mundane tasks. I encourage everyone to consider how AI can be applied in your own environments—whether it’s in automating routine tasks or enhancing decision-making through data insights. 

Let’s continue to share our experiences and learnings in this space. Together, we can foster a community that embraces innovation while ensuring responsible AI use. What successes have you seen in your own implementations? I’d love to hear your stories!

actual_review_data.review_text: One of my good friends way back from college - Shivakumar Kn is looking out for new opportunities. He has more than 20 years experience in software development field, and has till recently worked as Application Development manager/solution architect. Please contact him if you have any openings which match his profile.
actual_review_data.product_description: Opportunities and job searching in the tech industry

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating the tech job market can feel overwhelming, especially with the rapid pace of change in our industry. As someone who has been on both sides of the hiring process, I want to share some insights that might help you in your job search. 

First, leverage your network. Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and even connections you’ve made at events like Microsoft Ignite. Personal referrals can significantly boost your chances of landing interviews. 

Second, tailor your resume and cover letter for each application. Highlight specific skills and experiences that align with the job description. Remember, it’s not just about listing your technical skills; it’s about demonstrating how you can solve problems for the company. 

Additionally, don’t shy away from showcasing your soft skills. Resilience, adaptability, and teamwork are just as crucial in tech roles as technical expertise. Employers are looking for candidates who can thrive in dynamic environments and contribute to a positive team culture. 

Lastly, take care of your mental health during this process. Job searching can be stressful, so make sure to set aside time for self-care and reach out for support when needed. Remember, you’re not alone in this journey. Let’s continue to support each other in our career transitions. 

If you have any tips or experiences to share, I’d love to hear them in the comments below! Together, we can navigate this landscape and find opportunities that align with our passions and skills.

8774ee3b-19ab-45ca-8197-b68720c4b1b8_rev.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

actual_review_data.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.
actual_review_data.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
As someone who has navigated the complexities of higher education in the U.S., I understand the challenges and opportunities that lie ahead for international students. Pursuing a degree here can be a transformative experience, but it’s essential to approach it with the right mindset and preparation. 

First, research is key. Understand the educational landscape, including the differences in academic systems, cultural nuances, and financial implications. Look for programs that align with your career goals and offer robust support for international students. 

Networking is equally important. Leverage platforms like LinkedIn to connect with alumni and current students. Their insights can be invaluable in helping you navigate your journey. Don’t hesitate to reach out for mentorship—many professionals are willing to share their experiences and advice. 

Additionally, consider the emotional aspects of this transition. It’s normal to feel overwhelmed or face impostor syndrome. Remember, you’re not alone in this journey. Engage with communities that support international students, and don’t shy away from seeking help when needed. 

Lastly, keep an open mind. Embrace the diversity and learning opportunities that come with studying in a new country. Each experience, whether positive or challenging, contributes to your personal and professional growth. 

Let’s support each other in this journey. If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Together, we can navigate the path to success in higher education and beyond!

corrected_prediction_data.review_text:
Navigating higher education in the U.S. can be both a transformative and challenging journey for international students. Here are some insights based on my experiences:

1. **Research Thoroughly**: Familiarize yourself with the educational landscape, including differences in academic systems and cultural nuances. Identify programs that align with your career aspirations and offer strong support for international students.

ab1fb966-d14e-4639-b504-00f7b6f9084b_rev.product_description: Exploration of personal challenges impacting careers

actual_review_data.review_text:
Estatisticas mundias estimam que "aprox. 70% das pessoas irão vivenciar sindrome de impostor pelo menos uma vez na vida" ( Essa experiência afeta principalmente as mulheres e, caso não identificada e persistente, pode ter impacto considerável em nossa carreira. 
A comunidade Brasil do The WIT Network faz um convite a você para participar de um bate papo online sobre o tema. Venha conhecer mais sobre a Sindrome de Impostor, bem como ouvir as estorias e dicas de profissionais talentosos que vivenciaram e superaram essa experiencia. 
Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women. 
Non identified cases or persistently experiencing Imposter Syndrome may affect your career progression. With this in mind, the Brazilian Community of The WIT Network invites you to join an online roundtable about the subject. Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

actual_review_data.review_text:
In the last week I decided to close a cycle in my life. I finished a fantastic journey of 15 years working on research and development at Eldorado. 
Thank you Eldorado friends for all support and great work that has changed so many customers' business, that has contributed to show that it is possible to do innovation and research in Brazil.
Thank you also to all partners and customers that gave me the chance to learn with you.
Now, it is time to start a new journey full of new innovation challenges. 
 Let's empower every people to innovate.
#newjourney #gratefulness #innovation

881ba53e-5c01-4ae4-bc09-59d059c315a5_rev.product_description: Engagement and support for data engineering careers in Latin America

actual_review_data.review_text:
If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Last year I embarked on a mission to help people understand more about Data Engineering. I had the opportunity to talk to almost 30 people about data engineering and share some insights about the profession with them.

I've decided again to open some slots on my schedule this year! :)
So, if you are into Data Engineering and you are interested to join Factored or you want to get some guidance on Data Engineering, please reach out to me. Also, if you know anyone interested in this topic, please tag them so we can get in touch! :)

You can find the link to grab a slot on my calendar in the comments :)
#factored #dataengineering #data #latinamerica

actual_review_data.review_text:
Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

actual_review_data.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

actual_review_data.review_text:
I rarely reshare content, but this one deeply resonated.

Simon Sinek nails what it means to truly lead. He also nails what it means to truly love.

It's a rare thing that someone can point out something so obviously true but yet so fleeting that you immediately wanna say “This makes so much sense how did I not see this”.

This video also made me very aware of all the little things I am doing, I am not doing and I should be doing. Especially concerning showing appreciation for certain people. 

This video made me reach out to a person from a very vulnerable place and it rekindled a deep connection.

What are some small ways in which you can better show your love / leadership (pick one)?
actual_review_data.product_description: Insights on leadership and relationship building through personal reflection

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey as a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft, I've come to realize that leadership is not just about guiding teams or making decisions—it's about building meaningful relationships. Throughout my career, I've learned that the strongest connections often stem from vulnerability and authenticity. Sharing my experiences, whether it’s celebrating a Hackathon win or navigating the challenges of balancing my passions as a pianist and engineer, has allowed me to foster a supportive community around me. 

Embracing the emotional aspects of our careers, such as resilience and mental well-being, is crucial for personal growth and team dynamics. It's essential to create spaces where we can share our stories, learn from one another, and uplift those around us. 

As we continue to innovate and push boundaries in tech, let’s not forget the power of our relationships. They are the backbone of our success and the key to creating inclusive environments where everyone can thrive. How do you prioritize relationship building in your leadership journey? I’d love to hear your thoughts and experiences! #Leadership #WomenInTech #CommunitySupport #Resilience

actual_review_data.review_text:
Just finished the course “Learning React.js”! #reactjs
Made my first web app UI with React.

Look out for these key concepts:
Creating and rendering React elements,
Representing UI elements with React components,
adding props to display dynamic data,
Building components with JS class,
Using React states to re-render and change state of components,
bind event functions to UI elements with state variables,
and Conditional rendering of components.

JSX is a boon! Makes writing your own components much easier and makes my source much cleaner.

Eve Porcello does a great job of breaking down React concepts, and a hands-on example to help you build on your own.

Whether you're diving into AI, cloud computing, or full-stack development, the right frameworks can significantly enhance your productivity and innovation. 

I recently explored several frameworks that have transformed my approach to projects, particularly in the realm of AI and automation. For instance, integrating TensorFlow with cloud services like Azure has not only streamlined my workflow but also opened up new avenues for deploying machine learning models at scale. 

Here are a few actionable tips for those looking to enhance their skills with programming frameworks:
1. **Start Small**: Choose a framework that aligns with your current projects. For example, if you're working with data, consider starting with Pandas or TensorFlow.
2. **Hands-On Practice**: Build small projects to apply what you learn. This could be as simple as creating a basic app or automating a task.
3. **Leverage Community Resources**: Engage with online communities, attend webinars, or join local meetups. Sharing knowledge and experiences can accelerate your learning.
4. **Stay Updated**: Technology evolves rapidly. Follow industry leaders and subscribe to newsletters to keep abreast of the latest trends and best practices.

Frameworks like React and Django not only streamline the development process but also enhance collaboration among teams. Here are a few actionable tips to get started:

1. **Choose the Right Framework**: Consider your project requirements and team expertise. React is fantastic for dynamic user interfaces, while Django excels in rapid development with its robust backend capabilities.

2. **Focus on Component Reusability**: In React, build reusable components to maintain consistency and reduce redundancy. This practice not only saves time but also improves maintainability.

3. **Integrate AI Features**: Don’t forget to explore AI integrations! Tools like TensorFlow can enhance your web applications with intelligent features, making them more interactive and user-friendly.

4. **Prioritize Security**: Always keep security in mind, especially when handling user data. Implement best practices like input validation and secure authentication.

Let’s embrace the power of these frameworks to build innovative solutions that can transform industries. What frameworks have you found most effective in your projects? Share your thoughts below! 💬

4) An underlying HD spatial semantic map (including lanes, crosswalks, etc.)

🚗💡 Excited to share some valuable resources for those venturing into the world of autonomous vehicles! Open source datasets are a game-changer for developers and researchers alike, providing the foundational data needed to train and test AI models effectively. 

Here are a few noteworthy datasets to kickstart your projects:
1. **KITTI Dataset** - A comprehensive dataset for 3D object detection, tracking, and segmentation.
2. **Cityscapes Dataset** - Perfect for semantic segmentation tasks in urban environments.
3. **ApolloScape** - Offers a diverse range of data for various driving scenarios.
4. **Udacity Self-Driving Car Dataset** - A great resource for beginners, complete with labeled images and driving logs.

Leveraging these datasets not only accelerates development but also fosters collaboration within our community. As we push the boundaries of AI in transportation, let’s remember the importance of responsible AI practices and inclusivity in tech.

actual_review_data.review_text:
“The battle for #artificialintelligence supremacy is a binary game between US & China ”— this is what the media likes you to believe. 

My last talk in Berlin debunked exactly this propagated myth. 

I had highlighted that AI innovation can and will come from anywhere since:
 1) talent, 
2)Infrastructure and 
3) AI tech & tools 
are accessible to anyone with internet and hunger to solve a problem. 

Do you agree?

f20ab826-b48b-42cf-be34-de640b4befa1_rev.product_description: AI impact on healthcare diagnostics and research

actual_review_data.review_text:
Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

f20ab826-b48b-42cf-be34-de640b4befa1_rev.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

actual_review_data.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.
